commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Therefore I will lament and wail, I will go stripped and naked: I will make a wailing like the jackals, and mourning like the ostriches. Ill clothed, (Haydock) to show the approaching calamity of the Israelites, Isaias xx. (Menochius) Septuagint and Chaldean explain all of the people, (Calmet) or of Samaria. "Therefore shall she lament and howl, go barefoot and naked, bewail like "(Haydock) Dragons, when they are crushed by the elephant. (Solin xxxviii. ) (Menochius) Tannim means also (Haydock) whales, which make a horrible noise. Ostriches, or swans, Isaias xiii. Both have a mournful note.
